title: ovieda fasciculata
intro: Ovieda fasciculata, also known as the fascicled ovieda or wild marjoram, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving digestion, and fighting off respiratory infections. Ovieda fasciculata can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a topical application for wounds and skin irritations.
